Article Title: Arvid Lindblad interview: Playing cricket at Mumbai maidans, Indian roots, F1 journey, Verstappen bond
Article Text:
Arvid Lindblad (Image credit: AP)

Excerpts:

You scored points in your very first race. It must have been an emotional moment - can you take us back to that memory?

What was it like lining up against drivers you grew up watching as icons of the sport? How did it feel racing alongside them?

It must have been a special moment for your family as well. What were those conversations like?

You share a great bond with Max Verstappen. That viral video of him asking for your driving licence caught a lot of attention - what was that moment like?

Three races into your career now - what are the biggest lessons you’ve learned so far?

You’ve often spoken about Oliver Rowland. What role has he played in your journey?

If you hadn’t pursued professional racing, what do you think you’d be doing today?

Can you tell us a bit about your journey with Red Bull - from the junior programme to now being on the F1 grid?

How was your recent trip to Mumbai?

You played cricket at the Mumbai maidans, where Sachin Tendulkar began his journey. What was that experience like?

You also visited Mannat, home to Shah Rukh Khan. How was that visit?

NEW DELHI: When Arvid Lindblad sat in his Racing Bulls car at the Australian Grand Prix earlier this year, there was already buzz about a young driver with Indian roots making his debut alongside stars like Max Verstappen, Fernando Alonso and Lewis Hamilton. But once the chequered flag came down, all eyes were on the 18-year-old, who went on to score points for his team on debut.Lindblad finished eighth and picked up four points for Racing Bulls. Jumping out of his car, celebrating with a roar, and soaking in the moment, his emotions were evident after an impressive debut.Recently, Lindblad took time off to explore India as he tried Mumbai’s street food, visited popular spots, interacted with locals, and even stopped by Mannat. The 18-year-old spoke to TimesofIndia.com in an exclusive interview about his Formula One journey so far, his connection with India, and much more.Yeah, it was very special - very emotional. It’s something I’ve dreamed of my whole life, making it to Formula One. To finally have that moment in Australia, with both my parents there, made it even more meaningful. The whole weekend went so smoothly - getting into Q3, scoring points, and even running in the top three at one stage on the opening lap was pretty surreal.I absolutely loved every bit of it. It’s a day I’ll remember forever.Yeah, it was pretty surreal. Racing alongside guys like Fernando Alonso and Lewis Hamilton - and even Max Verstappen, who really rose through the sport around the time I started following it - was incredibly special. These are drivers I’ve watched for years on TV, so to be out there racing wheel-to-wheel with them is hard to put into words. I’d say Lewis was the one I was probably most starstruck by at the beginning, and even now there are still plenty of ‘pinch me’ moments. Even in Japan, during the drivers’ parade, it felt pretty special just being there alongside him. But once I’m on track, with the helmet on, it’s different - we’re all competitors, and I’m going to take every opportunity I can. Still, it’s amazing. I remember even last year during FP1 sessions, hearing on the radio, ‘Hamilton coming through’ when he was on a push lap - it’s pretty special just to hear that and to share the track with him.Yeah, it really was special. This has always been my dream and my journey - something I’ve been working towards for as long as I can remember. But it’s not just mine, it’s something we’ve all sacrificed for as a family. My parents, even my brother - they’ve all played a part in this. So it truly felt like a family moment.That’s why having them there with me in Melbourne made it even more meaningful. It’s hard to describe, but I remember after the race, once I got out of the car and left the media pen, my parents were waiting for me in hospitality. The first thing I did was just hug them. It’s something we’ve dreamed about for years - especially me and my dad.For so long, it felt so far away. We were in the UK, racing on small tracks, waking up at 6 in the morning in the cold and rain. My dad was my mechanic, and we were doing everything ourselves. It didn’t always feel real that we’d get here one day. And then suddenly, it is real. It’s happening. That moment just hit us. We didn’t say much - we just hugged, got a bit emotional. I almost had tears in my eyes after the race. It was a very special feeling.(Laughs) Yeah, it was pretty cool. I’ve known Max Verstappen for a while now, and he’s always been extremely friendly to me. I have a lot of respect for him - not just as a driver, because the level he performs at is incredible, but also as a person.He’s always been very kind, approachable, and willing to offer advice whenever I’ve needed it. What stands out is how humble and grounded he is - he hasn’t let the fame or spotlight change him, which is really great to see. In that sense, he’s definitely someone I look up to. I’ve been fortunate to spend time with him and build a good rapport.That viral moment was from a filming day a few months ago, when it came up that I didn’t have my driving licence yet. It was a funny situation, and something we could both relate to, having entered Formula One at a very young age. I think both of our journeys have been quite similar in that sense - we progressed quickly, came into F1 young, and have been part of the Red Bull setup. So it’s been really nice to share those experiences and moments with him.I think it’s been a massive change in Formula One this year with the new regulations - new engines, new cars, new tyres - so there’s been a lot to learn and get up to speed with. For me, the biggest challenge right now is managing everything. I already had a sense of it from last year, through TPC running and FP1 sessions, that in F1 you have a lot more influence over the car.There are many more tools available and a lot more you can adapt compared to other categories. In junior series, it’s pretty much just you and the car - you can’t change much. But in F1, there’s a lot more performance to unlock, and this year even more so, especially on the power unit side. The way you drive - your inputs - can affect how and when you get the power, and how it’s delivered.So there are a lot more variables you can control, and the key is to use them in the right way at the right time. Managing all of that, especially during a race when so much is happening, hasn’t been easy. It’s something all drivers are adapting to because it’s quite different, but as a rookie, I feel there’s even more performance for me to unlock compared to the others.Yeah, Oli has been a massive help for me - someone I’ve known for a very long time. I first met him when I was seven, and we actually started karting together. He supported me a lot when I was racing in Europe - coming to races, staying in touch, and guiding me along the way. In Formula 3 and Formula 2, he became even more involved.He attended many races and played a big role in my development. His connection with Red Bull has also been a key factor in helping me progress quickly through the ranks and reach Formula One. It’s actually quite hard to describe his role because it’s a bit of everything. He’s a mentor, but also feels like family given how long we’ve known each other.He helps me across all aspects of being a driver. As you know, the role today isn’t just about driving. He works with me on performance, gives feedback on what I can improve, but also helps with things like preparing for race weekends, handling media, and managing everything that comes with the job. So it’s not just one thing - he’s been there to guide me in every aspect of my journey.I’m not entirely sure, but I’ve always loved sports and I’m quite a driven, competitive person. So I think I would have ended up pursuing some kind of sport. Maybe even cricket, who knows? But honestly, it’s hard to say - I started karting when I was five, so this path began very early for me. I’m pretty sure I would have been involved in sport in some way.”You have a unique heritage and a strong connection to India through your family. Has that influenced your identity or the way you approach your career? And what do you enjoy most about your visits to India?Yeah, I’m very proud of my Indian heritage. It’s been a big part of my upbringing and has really shaped me into the person and driver I am today. From a young age, I’ve been connected to it - celebrating traditions, doing puja with my grandparents, and enjoying my nanny’s Indian food. I still remember when she used to come to the racetrack when I was seven or eight, bringing home-cooked meals - we’d be having rajma chawal, chapatis, and channa at the back of the car.Those are really special memories, and they’ve kept me closely connected to my roots. It’s something I’ve spoken about more recently, but it’s always been important to me. The values - hard work, humility, always giving your best - have had a big influence on who I am today. I always enjoy coming to India. Like you mentioned, the puja we did together in Delhi before the show run, where she kind of wished me luck before my first race in Melbourne, was really special. It’s those little things that mean a lot. We share a very close bond, and I really value that connection.Yeah, obviously, I’m very grateful to Red Bull for the support they’ve given me. I was signed to the programme when I was 13, at the end of 2020, and since then, things have come a long way - it’s been a really special journey. I still remember how it all started, meeting Helmut Marko (former Austrian F1 driver) in a hotel when he told me he wanted to sign me.I was incredibly excited, especially knowing he had backed drivers like Sebastian Vettel and Max Verstappen, who went on to achieve great things. For him to show that kind of belief in me meant a lot. Since then, both Helmut and ‘Rocky’ have played huge roles in my development. Rocky, especially on the technical side, has been someone I’ve leaned on a lot. His experience, including working closely with Sebastian and winning world championships, has helped me learn and improve as a driver.With Helmut, I’ve always had a very honest relationship. We speak openly about what’s going well and what needs improvement, and that’s really helped me progress quickly. It’s thanks to them, and the entire Red Bull Junior Programme, that I’ve been able to rise through the ranks so fast and reach Formula One. I’ll always be extremely grateful for that opportunity - especially to Helmut, who played a big part in me getting this chance. I wouldn’t be here without that support.I had a lot of fun visiting Mumbai - going to Mannat, spending time at the beaches, interacting with kids, and playing cricket with them at the maidans where Sachin Tendulkar started his career. It was a really enjoyable experience. It was my second visit to India - we had come in December 2025 as well - but this was my first time in Mumbai, and I absolutely loved it. It was great to learn more about Indian culture and explore the city.We did a mix of touristy things and fun activities. We visited Mannat, went to the Gateway of India, and explored a lot around the city. I even tried vada pav for the first time, which was amazing. Overall, it was a fantastic trip - we did so many things and really got the full experience. I loved every bit of it and I’m really looking forward to coming back later this year.To be honest, I don’t know if I’m at a level where I can say I played proper shots - I was mostly just trying to hit the ball as hard as I could. But it was great fun. The kids were incredibly friendly. When we were playing at the maidans, there were people of all ages - adults in their 20s and 30s, even older, and young kids around six or seven.Everyone was very welcoming and happy to let me join in. I got a chance to stand in front of the wickets, do some batting, and just enjoy the moment. It was a really fun experience playing with everyone.I mean, obviously, Shah Rukh Khan is a massive Bollywood star - more like a legend, to be honest. I can’t quite remember the name of the film now, but I do recall a scene we tried to recreate when I was in Delhi a couple of months ago.We did a fun social concept where we were sort of copying that iconic moment when he comes home and meets his mother. I did the walk-up, and then we had that warm embrace and hug. I don’t remember the exact dialogue, but the scene itself is really famous, and it was great fun trying to recreate it.
Article Summary: Arvid lindblad made his formula one debut at the australian grand prix. The 18-year-old finished eighth and picked up four points for racing bulls. He took time off to explore india as he tried street food, visited popular spots, interacted with locals, and even stopped by mannat.

Article Title: MI IPL 2026 playoff qualification scenarios: Time is running out for Mumbai Indians after Chepauk collapse
Article Text:
The Mumbai Indians' dreams of IPL 2026 playoff qualification are hanging by a thread following an emphatic eight-wicket defeat to the Chennai Super Kings. The alarming trend of middle-order collapses, most recently in the battle against CSK, has been the story of MI this IPL 2026, and that has now propelled the 5-time champions into a do-or-die situation.

Mumbai Indians' current points table standing

Matches played: 9

Wins: 2

Losses: 7

Points: 4

Net Run Rate: -0.803

Position: 9th

Watch Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions

IPL 2026 playoff qualification benchmark

14 points (7 wins): Minimum realistic cutoff

16 points (8 wins): Safe qualification zone

What Mumbai Indians need from here

Matches remaining: 5

Current points: 4

To reach 14 points: Need 5 wins from 5 matches

To reach 16 points: Need 6 wins (not possible anymore)

The big problem: The misfiring middle-order

Upcoming IPL 2026 fixtures for Mumbai Indians

May 10 vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Raipur - must-win against a top-two side

May 14 vs Punjab Kings, Dharamsala - tough away test against table leaders

May 20 vs Kolkata Knight Riders, Kolkata - direct clash with another struggling side

May 24 vs Rajasthan Royals, Mumbai - against a top-four contender

Mumbai Indians’ campaign took another decisive hit as they slumped to an eight-wicket defeat against Chennai Super Kings at Chepauk, a result that has pushed them to the brink of elimination. After cruising past 100 in the 11th over, MI once again imploded, collapsing to 159/7 despite a fluent start from Suryakumar Yadav and a fighting 57 from Naman Dhir. CSK chased the target with ease, led by Ruturaj Gaikwad’s composed unbeaten 67. The loss not only exposed MI’s recurring batting issues but has now left their playoff hopes hanging by the thinnest of threads.Mumbai Indians are languishing near the bottom of the table with just two wins from nine matches, and their negative NRR only compounds the damage. With very little margin left, they are already in deep trouble.Historically, the qualification cutoff in the IPL has remained consistent.The only exception came in 2019, when Sunrisers Hyderabad qualified with 12 points — the only instance of a team progressing with fewer than 14 points and more losses than wins.In most seasons, 7 wins keeps you alive, 8 wins puts you in control.Mumbai Indians now require a perfect run - 5 wins from 5 games - just to stay in contention for 14 points.One more loss and they are almost out.MI's run through the season has been a campaign that never found stability. Seven defeats in nine matches and what stands out is the nature of these losses. Several have come from winning positions - including the latest collapse against CSK where they went from a strong platform to a below-par total. The middle order has repeatedly failed to convert starts, and the inability to accelerate in the final overs has hurt them badly.Their bowling has had moments of control, but the lack of scoreboard pressure has often left them defending sub-par totals. Overall, MI have struggled to put together a complete performance across departments.Mumbai Indians’ biggest issue this season has not been their bowling or even their starts with the bat, it is what happens after the 10th over.Time and again, MI have built solid platforms only to lose momentum through clusters of wickets. Against CSK, they were well placed at over 100 in 11 overs, projecting a total beyond 180. Instead, they managed just 159/7. This pattern has repeated across multiple games. MI’s run rate in the middle overs has dipped significantly compared to the powerplay, and their boundary percentage drops sharply after the first 10 overs. More crucially, they have lost wickets in quick succession, turning strong positions into recovery phases.Even in the best-case scenario, MI will be reliant on other results and a significant NRR boost. Mumbai Indians are not out mathematically, but realistically, they are hanging by a thread. With five must-win games and no room for slip-ups, their path to the playoffs demands a near-perfect turnaround. Given their recurring middle-order failures and inconsistency, that looks increasingly unlikely. Time is running out - and fast.
Article Summary: Mumbai indians' dreams of ipl 2026 playoff qualification are hanging by a thread following an eight-wicket defeat to the chennai super kings. Mumbai indians are languishing near the bottom of the table with just two wins from nine matches. They now require a perfect run - 5 wins from 5 games - just to stay in contention for 14 points.

Article Title: IPL 2026 playoffs venues to be decided in a day or two: Devajit Saikia
Article Text:
IPL trophy (PTI Photo)

Mumbai:

Watch Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions

The BCCI will announce the venues of the IPL 2026 playoffs in a couple of days' time. "We are working on what could be the appropriate venue for the IPL playoffs and the final. So, we will take another two days' time," BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia told reporters at the BCCI HQ here on Saturday, just after announcing the team for the 2026 Women's T20 World Cup.The BCCI secretary refused to divulge if the designated venues - Mullanpur and Bengaluru - by virtue of Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Punjab Kings being the finalists and champions last year, will host the playoff ties. "You can keep guessing, but I am not going to give you a reply. We will take a call in two days' time," he said.Mullanpur hosted Qualifier 1 and the Eliminator, while the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad (Eden Gardens in Kolkata was supposed to stage the final as per convention) staged Qualifier 2 and the final out of turn in last year's season, which was halted for a while due to the Indo-Pak war.The BCCI has not released the dates of the IPL 2026 playoffs, except announcing that the final will be held on May 31.
Article Summary: The bcci will announce the venues of the ipl 2026 playoffs in a couple of days' time. The designated venues - mullanpur and bengaluru - by virtue of royal challengers bengaluru and punjab kings being the finalists and champions last year, will host the playoff ties. The final will be held on may 31.

Article Title: “Trevor from GTA V?” – Fans stunned as Gonzalo Higuaín, once Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi’s rival, looks unrecognisable in rare public appearance
Article Text:
Gonzalo Higuaín (via X)

Fans react to Gonzalo Higuain’s shocking transformation

Football fans around the world were left stunned after a recent photo of former striker Gonzalo Higuaín surfaced online. The ex-Real Madrid and Argentina star has stayed away from the spotlight since retiring in 2022. Because of his long absence from social media and public events, many people were surprised to see how different he now looks compared to his playing days.The 38-year-old was spotted in Miami posing casually with a fan. The image quickly went viral, with thousands of reactions pouring in across social media. Many users struggled to recognise the once-famous striker. His new look, featuring a rugged beard and a more relaxed style, sparked debate among fans. His fans remember him as one of the sharpest forwards of his generation.The viral image triggered strong reactions online, with many fans expressing disbelief at how much Gonzalo Higuaín has changed. A fan wrote, "That's Trevor from GTA V", hinting that he looked more like a video game character than a former football star. Another wrote, "what happened to him?", while others questioned how drastically his appearance had changed in just a few years.A few comments even compared him to a rough-looking character, suggesting he appeared far removed from his polished playing days. One wrote, "He used to give fight to Ronaldo and Messi, what happened to him". Some fans even reacted with shocked emojis.While many former football stars like Thierry Henry, Zlatan Ibrahimović, and David Beckham have stayed super fit and stylish even after retirement, Gonzalo Higuaín seems to be enjoying life at a much slower pace. He now prefers comfort over style. His relaxed look is very different from his previous sharp clean image.But even though his appearance has changed, his football career still speaks for itself. Higuaín played over 700 matches and scored more than 300 goals. He started his journey at River Plate before making a big move to Europe. There, he played for top clubs like Napoli, Juventus, and AC Milan. He even had a short stint at Chelsea under Maurizio Sarri.He finally called time on his career with Inter Miami at the age of 34. So yes, his latest look may have caught fans off guard, but what he achieved on the field is what truly defines him and that hasn’t changed at all.
Article Summary: Gonzalo higuaín was spotted in miami posing casually with a fan. The 38-year-old's new look, featuring a rugged beard and a more relaxed style, sparked debate among fans. Many users struggled to recognise the once-famous striker.

Article Title: India's T20 World Cup squad: Why were Kashvee Gautam and Amanjot Kaur not available for selection?
Article Text:
Kashvee Gautam (Getty Images)

India Women’s squad for 2026 Women’s T20 World Cup and T20I series vs England

India Women’s squad for one-off Test against England at Lord’s

NEW DELHI: India’s squad announcement for the 2026 Women’s T20 World Cup brought a few surprises, but one of the biggest talking points was the absence of all-rounders Kashvee Gautam and Amanjot Kaur.The BCCI clarified on Saturday that both players were unavailable for selection because of injuries, forcing the selectors to rethink India’s pace-bowling depth ahead of the marquee event in England and Wales beginning June 12.With two promising seam-bowling options ruled out, the selectors turned towards uncapped pacer Nandini Sharma, who earned her maiden World Cup call-up after an impressive run in domestic cricket and the Women’s Premier League.Representing Delhi Capitals Women in WPL 2026, Nandini picked up 17 wickets in 10 matches and emerged as one of the standout Indian bowlers of the tournament.She was also part of India’s squad for the Women’s Asia Cup Rising Stars competition in Bangkok earlier this year, where she continued to impress with her pace and control.India have also welcomed back wicketkeeper-batter Yastika Bhatia, who returns after recovering from the ACL injury she suffered during the ODI World Cup preparatory camp last year.Her inclusion gives the squad additional depth behind the stumps alongside Richa Ghosh.Meanwhile, Pratika Rawal missed out on the T20 World Cup squad despite being in contention for the England tour.However, the selectors have retained faith in the young batter by including her in the squad for the one-off Test against England at Lord’s from July 10 to 13.Led by Harmanpreet Kaur, with Smriti Mandhana as vice-captain, India’s World Cup squad will first play a three-match T20I series against England starting May 28 as preparation for the global tournament.Harmanpreet Kaur (C), Smriti Mandhana (VC), Shafali Verma, Jemimah Rodrigues, Bharti Fulmali, Deepti Sharma, Richa Ghosh (wk), Shree Charani, Yastika Bhatia, Nandini Sharma, Renuka Singh Thakur, Arundhati Reddy, Kranti Gaud, Shreyanka Patil, Radha Yadav.Harmanpreet Kaur (C), Smriti Mandhana (VC), Shafali Verma, Jemimah Rodrigues, Pratika Rawal, Deepti Sharma, Richa Ghosh (wk), Shree Charani, Yastika Bhatia (wk), Nandini Sharma, Harleen Deol, Renuka Singh Thakur, Kranti Gaud, Sayali Satghare, Sneh Rana.
Article Summary: All-rounders kashvee gautam and amanjot kaur ruled out of the squad. Nandini sharma named in the squad for the 2026 women’s t20 world cup. India will play a three-match t20i series against england starting may 28.

Article Title: IPL 2026: No MS Dhoni or Rohit Sharma! CSK, MI name changes in playing XIs
Article Text:
MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma (BCCI Photo)

Watch Kumar Sangakkara on RR’s win over Punjab Kings Kumar Sangakkara on RR’s win over Punjab Kings

Chennai Super Kings (Playing XI)

Impact Subs:

Mumbai Indians (Playing XI):

Impact Subs:

NEW DELHI: The spotlight may still be on the iconic rivalry between Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians , but IPL 2026 served up a surprise even before the first ball was bowled. There was no MS Dhoni and no Rohit Sharma in the playing XIs. Instead, a new-look CSK and MI walked out with fresh combinations and several under-pressure stars looking to rediscover form.Mumbai Indians won the toss and opted to bat first, with Hardik Pandya saying the team wanted to “give another opportunity to the batters to score runs” after an inconsistent campaign.MI also handed a debut to young leg-spinner Raghu Sharma, while Krish Bhagat returned to the XI.Chennai Super Kings skipper Ruturaj Gaikwad admitted he would have preferred to bat first but backed his side to adapt on what he described as a dry surface.CSK made two changes, bringing in Ramakrishna Ghosh and Prashant Veer in place of Gurjapneet Singh and Akeal Hosein.Despite the big names on both sides, several players have struggled this season. Jasprit Bumrah has managed only two wickets in eight matches, while both Gaikwad and Suryakumar Yadav have crossed fifty just once and scored under 200 runs so far.Hardik Pandya too has endured a below-par season with both bat and ball.CSK’s fortunes this year have largely revolved around Sanju Samson, whose runs at the top have powered most of their victories.Shivam Dube and Dewald Brevis are yet to consistently make an impact, while Sarfaraz Khan has failed to convert starts into meaningful scores.Mumbai’s issues have been even deeper. Despite three individual centuries this season, only one has come in a winning cause.Their bowling has struggled badly, with MI picking up only 38 wickets in eight games and conceding runs at a tournament-worst economy of 10.90.: Sanju Samson(w), Ruturaj Gaikwad(c), Urvil Patel, Dewald Brevis, Shivam Dube, Jamie Overton, Ramakrishna Ghosh, Prashant Veer, Noor Ahmad, Anshul Kamboj, Mukesh ChoudharyKartik Sharma, Akeal Hosein, Sarfaraz Khan, Matthew Short, Gurjapneet SinghWill Jacks, Ryan Rickelton(w), Suryakumar Yadav, Naman Dhir, Tilak Varma, Hardik Pandya(c), Robin Minz, Trent Boult, Krish Bhagat, Jasprit Bumrah, AM GhazanfarRaj Bawa, Raghu Sharma, Mayank Rawat, Shardul Thakur, Ashwani Kumar
Article Summary: Mumbai indians won the toss and opted to bat first against chennai super kings. Csk made two changes, bringing in ramakrishna ghosh and prashant veer. Mi also handed a debut to young leg-spinner raghu sharma, while krish bhagat returned to the xi.

Article Title: BAN vs NZ, 3rd T20I: Bevon Jacobs stars as New Zealand bounce back to beat Bangladesh by 6 wickets, level series
Article Text:
Bevon Jacobs (AP Photo)

Watch Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more

Bangladesh collapse after rain break

NEW DELHI: New Zealand bounced back from a shaky start to beat Bangladesh by six wickets in the rain-hit third T20 on Saturday and level the three-match series 1-1. The second game had earlier been washed out after Bangladesh won the opener by six wickets.Chasing a revised target of 103 in 15 overs, New Zealand were in serious trouble early at 25-3 after Bangladesh pacer Shoriful Islam ripped through the top order. Stand-in captain Nick Kelly also fell soon after, leaving the hosts struggling at 33-4.But Bevon Jacobs turned the game around with a brilliant unbeaten 62 off just 31 balls. His knock included five fours and three sixes as he guided New Zealand to 104-4 in 11.4 overs.Bangladesh captain Litton Das kept attacking with Shoriful, but Jacobs responded aggressively and smashed him for back-to-back boundaries to shift momentum. Jacobs reached his fifty in 29 balls and finished the match in style with a boundary and a six. Shoriful still ended with impressive figures of 3-19.Earlier, Bangladesh were asked to bat first but their innings never really settled. They had already slipped to 50-3 in 6.4 overs before rain stopped play for more than two hours, reducing the contest to 15 overs per side.After the restart, Bangladesh tried to attack every delivery, but the plan backfired badly. They lost their final seven wickets for just 39 runs in 35 balls and were bowled out for 102 in 14.2 overs.Towhid Hridoy top-scored with 33, while Litton Das made 26 and Saif Hassan added 16. No other batter could reach double figures.Josh Clarkson starred with the ball for New Zealand, taking a career-best 3-9 in two overs. Ben Sears and Nathan Smith picked up two wickets each, while Ish Sodhi claimed one wicket to become New Zealand’s leading T20I wicket-taker with 165 wickets, surpassing Tim Southee’s 164.
Article Summary: New zealand beat bangladesh by six wickets to level three-match series 1-1. The second game had earlier been washed out after bangladesh won the opener. Bevon jacobs turned the game around with a brilliant unbeaten 62 off just 31 balls as he guided new zealand to 104-4 in 11.4 overs.

Article Title: IPL 2026 Points Table after Match 43: DC move up, RR's erratic campaign continues
Article Text:
DC beat RR by 7 wickets (Pic credit: IPL)

Watch Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions

IPL 2026 Points Table after RR vs DC match:

Position Team M W L NR Points NRR 1 PBKS 8 6 1 1 13 +1.043 2 RCB 9 6 3 0 12 +1.420 3 SRH 9 6 3 0 12 +0.832 4 RR 10 6 4 0 12 +0.510 5 GT 9 5 4 0 10 -0.192 6 DC 9 4 5 0 8 -0.895 7 CSK 8 3 5 0 6 -0.121 8 KKR 8 2 5 1 5 -0.751 9 MI 8 2 6 0 4 -0.784 10 LSG 8 2 6 0 4 -1.106

NEW DELHI: Delhi Capitals registered their highest successful chase in IPL history against Rajasthan Royals on Friday at the Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur to put a break on their hat-trick of defeats in IPL 2026. DC chased down a record 226 in just 19.1 overs to collect a well-deserved two points.The win propelled DC to sixth spot in the IPL 2026 points table with 8 points from nine games, overtaking Chennai Super Kings, who play Mumbai Indians at home on Saturday.RR's erratic campaign continued as they suffered their fourth defeat in 10 games. Despite the loss, RR remained in fourth spot in the standings, but their net run rate (NRR) dropped to +0.510.Punjab Kings remain atop the standings with 13 points from 8 games, followed by defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru in second with 12 points from nine games. Sunrisers Hyderabad are third with a superior NRR over RR, as all three teams -RCB, SRH and RR, have 12 points each.Gujarat Titans are fifth with 10 points as they march towards the playoff spots while consolidating their mid-table position. GT have reduced the gap with fourth-placed RR to just two points and are two points ahead of DC.The rest of the table remains unchanged. CSK are at seventh with six points, followed by Kolkata Knight Riders at eighth with five points. While, Mumbai Indians are ninth with four points, just ahead of bottom-placed Lucknow Super Giants on NRR.With the league stage entering a crucial phase, the playoff race is heating up. DC have kept themselves in contention with a crucial win against RR on Friday.
Article Summary: Delhi capitals registered their highest successful chase in ipl history against rajasthan royals on friday. Dc chased down a record 226 in just 19.1 overs to collect a well-deserved two points. The win propelled dc to sixth spot in the ipl 2026 points table with 8 points from nine games.

Article Title: Vanessa Bryant shares painful birthday message for Gianna as Kobe Bryant memories return and 20 scholarships revealed
Article Text:
Vanessa Bryant and Gianna Bryant (Image via Getty)

Vanessa Bryant and Gianna Bryant’s legacy lives on through scholarships and family tributes

The day was filled with love and pain for Vanessa Bryant as she marked what would have been her daughter’s 20th birthday. She shared a quiet but emotional post on Instagram, reminding everyone how much she still feels her daughter’s presence, even after all these years. The message was simple, but it carried deep emotion. “Happy birthday to my sweet baby angel, Gianna,” she wrote, alongside a photo of them together. “Words can’t express how much I love and miss you mamacita. Mommy loves you so much!”.The post quickly connected with fans who still remember the young basketball player’s bright future. Gianna Bryant passed away in January 2020 in a helicopter crash with her father, Kobe Bryant . They were on their way to a basketball game in California. She was only 13. The loss shook the sports world and continues to be remembered.This birthday was not just about remembrance. It also became a moment to celebrate Gianna’s dreams. The Mamba & Mambacita Sports Foundation announced something meaningful on the same day. In a video shared online, the foundation revealed that 20 scholarships were given to young athletes in Gianna’s name.The announcement said, “In Gigi’s honor, we are proud to announce the 2026 Class of Gianna Bryant Scholars, 20 exceptional student-athletes from Los Angeles and Orange County, administered in partnership with the California Community Foundation and local Boys & Girls Clubs of America affiliates.”The message ended with, “And to our Gigi, Happy 20th Birthday! Your vision, your spirit, and your beautiful heart continue to light the way. ”Vanessa has kept her daughter’s memory alive in many ways. She often shares family moments with her daughters Natalia, Bianka, and Capri. On this birthday, Natalia Bryant also posted photos of herself with Gianna and wrote, “Happy Birthday Gigi.”Just days earlier, Vanessa had shared photos with Kobe to mark their 25th wedding anniversary. She also remembered him on his birthday last year with another heartfelt message, writing, “We love and miss you and Gigi so much. Sending our love to you. Happy birthday, baby.”Through these moments, Vanessa continues to keep both Kobe and Gianna close, not just for her family but for fans around the world.
Article Summary: Vanessa bryant marked what would have been her daughter's 20th birthday. Gianna bryant passed away in january 2020 in a helicopter crash with her father, kobe bryant. The mamba & mambacita sports foundation announced 20 scholarships in gianna's name.

Article Title: Luke Littler challenges Mo Farah to wild half marathon with 501 darts twist as talks begin to heat up
Article Text:
Luke Littler and Mo Farah (Image Via Getty)

Luke Littler backs himself against Mo Farah in half marathon with darts twist as both stars react to viral challenge

A strange but fun challenge is now picking up attention, and it involves two very different sports coming together. Luke Littler has said yes to a viral idea where he could face Mo Farah in a half marathon. But this is not just about running. The twist is that both of them would need to hit a 501 checkout on a dartboard after every mile.The idea sounds unusual, but it has already caught the interest of fans and even other athletes. Littler spoke about it after his latest Premier League Darts win in Aberdeen. While he seemed excited, he also admitted he would need to get fitter before taking it on. At the same time, Mo Farah has also shown interest, which makes this challenge feel closer to becoming real.Luke Littler gave a very honest and relaxed response when asked about the challenge. He said, “I don’t think Mo Farah could take out 501 personally. So as soon as I am done, nine darter, I am walking my next mile. Yeah, I think we should do it for charity. Obviously let me lose a bit of timber and I’ll be good to go.”His confidence comes from his strength in darts, even though Farah is one of the best long-distance runners in the world.The format would make things interesting. After each mile, both would stop and try to finish a 501 score. That means focus and skill will matter just as much as stamina.Mo Farah, who is now 43, has also reacted positively. Responding to the idea, he said, “I will let my team get in touch with you! Yeeeezzzzzz!! 501.” That short reply was enough to show he is open to the challenge.Farah has a strong background in running. He won Olympic gold in both the 5,000 meters and 10,000 meters at the London 2012 and Rio 2016 Games. His personal best in a half marathon is 59 minutes and 22 seconds, which shows how strong he is over long distances.On the other side, Littler is enjoying a great season in darts. His recent win in Aberdeen was his fifth Premier League victory this season. That result has pushed him to the top of the standings, and he has already secured his place in the playoffs.The idea for this challenge also got support from boxer Dave Allen. Speaking on talkSPORT, he said he even tried to contact Farah to help make it happen.If the event goes ahead, both players would need to complete 13 successful 501 checkouts during the race. That mix of running and darts could make it one of the most unique sporting events fans have ever seen.
Article Summary: Luke littler has said yes to a viral idea where he could face mo farah in a half marathon. The twist is that both of them would need to hit a 501 checkout on a dartboard after every mile. The idea has already caught the interest of fans and even other athletes.

Article Title: Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce new part-owners of Kentucky Derby contender The Puma? Mystery deepens after NDA hint
Article Text:
Rumors suggest Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce may own a stake in Kentucky Derby contender The Puma, but nothing is confirmed. Speculation grew after co-owner Michael Iavarone said he could “neither confirm nor deny” due to an NDA. The buzz boosted the horse’s popularity and odds, with fans and bettors closely watching as the Derby approaches.

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Kentucky Derby rumors gain traction

Michael Iavarone’s cryptic post fuels Swift Kelce speculation

The speculation surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce has taken an unexpected turn, this time in the high stakes world of horse racing. What began as a murmur of online gossip soon became a roar after a big player linked to a Kentucky Derby hopeful jumped in with both feet. Now fans are watching closely, their curiosity turning into buzz.The intrigue centers on The Puma, a Derby upstart who has surprisingly become the subject of celebrity talk. While there is no official confirmation tying Swift and Kelce to the horse, a cryptic response from co-owner Michael Iavarone has only deepened the mystery and kept fans guessing.The rumor mill kicked into high gear earlier this week when posts began circulating online claiming Swift and Kelce had quietly secured a stake in The Puma. One widely shared message read, “Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift are now 10 percent part owners of Kentucky derby entry The Puma.” That single claim was enough to ignite intense interest, especially among Swift’s massive fan base.And as the chatter continued, more folks started to make the connections. Swift and Kelce’s dabbling in the sport of horse racing added another layer to their already headline-grabbing lives.But in the absence of confirmation, the possibility was enough to make The Puma one of the most talked about names ahead of the race.The conversation reached another level when Iavarone addressed the rumors directly, though without offering clarity. In a message shared online, he wrote, “Ok people. Everyone asking me if Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are partners on The Puma…” before adding, “All I can say is I signed an NDA so I can’t confirm or deny.” The statement, paired with a playful tone, left fans reading between the lines.That ambiguity had a real impact. The Puma’s odds saw a sharp rise midweek, moving from around 10-1 to 7-2 before stabilizing. Some believe the surge was driven by Swift’s fanbase, known for rallying behind anything connected to the pop icon. As one sports commentator noted, “Rumor has it that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have bought into The Puma. The Swifties could make this horse a top choice on Saturday.”Whether the rumors prove true or not, the buzz has already changed the narrative. The Puma is no longer just a contender. It is now part of a larger cultural moment where sports, celebrity, and speculation collide.
Article Summary: Rumors suggest taylor swift and travis kelce may own a stake in kentucky derby contender the puma. Speculation grew after co-owner michael iavarone said he could “neither confirm nor deny” due to an nda. The buzz boosted the horse’s popularity and odds, with fans and bettors closely watching.

NEW DELHI: Delhi Capitals opener KL Rahul scored his third half-century of the IPL 2026 season to jump to the top of the Orange Cap standings. His 75 against Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur on Friday took his overall tally this season to 433 runs, eight more than Sunrisers Hyderabad opener Abhishek Sharma.Rahul was outside the top five before the match in Jaipur, but the 40-ball knock helped him leapfrog an array of run-getters like Shubman Gill, Virat Kohli, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Heinrich Klaasen and Abhishek to snatch the Orange Cap.Rahul now has 433 runs from nine games at an average of 54.12 and a strike rate of 185.83, which includes one hundred and three fifties.Sooryavanshi also had a chance to go top, but a rare failure for the 15-year-old sensation — who was out for just 4 on Friday — left the RR opener fourth with 404 runs from 10 games. The teenager has scored his runs at a stunning strike rate of 237.64 this season, including one hundred and two fifties.Abhishek slipped to second with 425 runs from nine games at an average of 53.12 and a strike rate of 209.35, followed by teammate Klaasen in third. Klaasen has 414 runs from nine matches at an average of 59.14 and a strike rate of 157.41.RCB talisman Kohli completes the top five with 379 runs from nine games at an average of 54.14 and a strike rate of 165.50, including three half-centuries. Gill has dropped out of the top five and is now in sixth spot with 373 runs from eight matches.
Article Summary: Delhi capitals opener kl rahul scored his third half-century of the ipl 2026 season to jump to the top of the orange cap standings. His 75 against rajasthan royals in jaipur on friday took his overall tally this season to 433 runs, eight more than sunrisers hyderabad opener abhishek sharma.

Article Title: Kentucky Derby 2026: Full schedule, start time, horses list, when and where to watch, everything you need to know
Article Text:
Kentucky Derby 2026, Fulleffort (via Getty)

Kentucky Derby 2026 venue

Kentucky Derby 2026 when and where to watch?

Kentucky Derby 2026 field and post positions list

Kentucky Derby 2026 winner prize money

The 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby is set to take place on Saturday, May 2. A total of 20 horses will compete in one of the most famous races in horse racing history. This event is known for its rich tradition and high stakes. Fans have been keeping an eye on the race as the field and post positions have been confirmed. The excitement is building as top contenders prepare for the big race.Renegade will enter the race as the early favorite but faces a difficult challenge to kickoff the race from the first position. The inside gate has not produced a winner for decades. Meanwhile, The Puma has gained strong backing in the recent past. Recent history also shows that favorites do not always win this race. This makes the 2026 edition even more unpredictable and exciting.The race will take place at Churchill Downs in Louisville, the traditional home of the Kentucky Derby since it began in 1875. The event is always scheduled for the first Saturday in May. This tradition started in 1932 and has mostly continued since then.The race itself is expected to begin at around 6:57 p.m. ET. However, the exact timing can change slightly depending on pre-race arrangements.Coverage will run throughout the day. NBC will begin broadcasting in the afternoon, while earlier races and coverage will be available through streaming platforms.The field for this year includes strong contenders like Renegade, The Puma, Commandment, and So Happy. Renegade drew the first post position, which is historically difficult. The last winner from this position came in 1986.1. Renegade2. Albus3. Intrepido4. Litmus Test5. Right to Party6. Commandment7. Danon Bourbon8. So Happy9. The Puma10. Wonder Dean11. Incredibolt12. Chief Wallabee13. Silent Tactic (Scratch)14. Potente15. Emerging Market16. Pavlovian17. Six Speed18. Further Ado19. Golden Tempo20. Fulleffort (Scratch)21. Great White22. OcelliThe race also features a total prize purse of $5 million. The winner is expected to earn around $3.1 million. Whereas, the second-place finisher will receive close to $1 million. The prize money has surged significantly in recent years. The surged prize value has made the event even more competitive.

Article Title: IPL 2026: ‘Never dreamt of this’- KL Rahul stunned by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s audacious hitting
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and KL Rahul (Agency Image)

Watch Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions

Poll Which aspect of T20 cricket do you find most exciting? High-scoring matches Nail-biting finishes

Delhi Capitals’ KL Rahul has heaped praise on Rajasthan Royals ’ teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, calling his rise a sign of how T20 cricket is rapidly evolving with fearless young talent.Speaking on JioStar’s ‘Superstars’, Rahul reflected on the impact Sooryavanshi has already made at just 15 years of age.“It’s phenomenal what the new-age cricketers are doing. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has taken world cricket by storm. Two centuries at the age of 15 is something I never even dreamt of doing,” Rahul said.Sooryavanshi’s numbers back that statement. In IPL 2026, he has scored 400 runs in nine matches at an average of 44.44 and a strike rate of 238.09, including a century and two fifties. His tally of 37 sixes is the highest in the tournament so far, putting him just six away from breaking Abhishek Sharma ’s record of 42 sixes by an Indian in a single IPL season.Rahul also pointed to the broader shift in Indian cricket, highlighting how young players are now embracing an ultra-aggressive mindset and taking on even the very best without hesitation.“The amount of talent coming up in India is scary, especially in T20 cricket. These fearless prodigies are changing the way T20s are played. They don’t fear reputations or match situations,” he said.“Whether it’s a world-class bowler like Jasprit Bumrah or a seasoned campaigner like Pat Cummins , they go after every ball with the same intent. They don’t care if it’s the first over or the last.They just see the ball and want to hit it out of the park. That kind of mindset is rare, and it’s exactly what modern T20 cricket demands.”Sooryavanshi is also on the verge of another milestone. He has already smashed 99 sixes in just 27 T20 innings before turning 20, the most by any batter at that age. One more six will take him to a landmark 100, a feat that could stand for years.Rahul believes the IPL has played a massive role in shaping this new generation of power-hitters.“Five years ago, we weren’t producing as many six-hitting batters as England or Australia. Now, things have changed, thanks to the IPL,” he said.“These guys grew up wanting to hit sixes. When we were young, we had to learn to defend and leave the ball. Hitting in the air meant sitting out of nets. It was a different time. These youngsters practice six-hitting constantly. That’s why they’re so good.”With Sooryavanshi continuing to rewrite expectations at just 15, Rahul’s words underline a larger shift in the game, one where fearless hitting is no longer the exception but the norm.
Article Summary: Kl rahul has heaped praise on rajasthan royals’ teenage sensation vaibhav sooryavanshi. Rahul called his rise a sign of how t20 cricket is rapidly evolving with fearless young talent.rahul also pointed to the broader shift in indian cricket, highlighting how young players are now embracing an ultra-aggressive mindset.

Article Title: Steve McClaren reveals why he told Erik ten Hag to choose between him and Cristiano Ronaldo at Manchester United
Article Text:
Cristiano Ronaldo (via Getty)

Steve McClaren explains why Cristiano Ronaldo could not fit Erik Ten Hag’s system

Former England manager Steve McClaren has made a bold claim about Cristiano Ronaldo ’s final months at Manchester United . He said he advised Erik ten Hag early on that the club would need to make a tough choice between the star forward and the manager’s system. The situation developed during the 2022–23 season, when Ronaldo struggled to fit into Ten Hag’s high-pressing style. The tension later became public and eventually led to a dramatic exit.The Portuguese gave The Athletic an explosive interview where he openly criticized Ten Hag and admitted he did not respect him. Soon after, Manchester United terminated his contract, and Ronaldo joined Al-Nassr in early 2023. McClaren has now explained that the issues were visible long before the public fallout. He also highlighted how Ronaldo found it difficult to adapt to the physical demands required by the manager’s approach.Steve McClaren, who worked closely with Erik Ten Hag during that period, explained that the conflict was mainly about playing style and expectations. He said the manager wanted a very intense pressing system, especially from the forwards. According to McClaren, he told Ten Hag quite early that the situation would come down to a clear decision between sticking with Cristiano Ronaldo or fully committing to the new system.He added that Ronaldo was professional and generally behaved well, but he was not comfortable with the role he was being asked to perform. McClaren explained that the manager wanted him to press aggressively. He wanted him to lead the defensive effort and constantly make repeated runs. However, Ronaldo questioned whether such demands were realistic, especially considering his age and playing style at that stage of his career.McClaren also shared that he had direct conversations with Ronaldo about these expectations. He told the forward that if he wanted to stay in the team, he would need to follow the tactical instructions and work harder without the ball. But Ronaldo was not convinced and often argued that constant pressing was not necessary.The situation eventually became unsustainable. Ronaldo made only limited appearances under Ten Hag and struggled to make an impact. His departure marked the end of an era at Manchester United.Meanwhile, former teammate Antony recently spoke about Ronaldo’s influence off the field. He said Ronaldo often encouraged discussions beyond football, including life lessons and personal growth, which helped younger players develop both professionally and personally.
Article Summary: Steve mcclaren explains why cristiano ronaldo could not fit erik ten hag’s system at manchester united. The former england manager said the conflict was mainly about playing style and expectations. He said the manager wanted a very intense pressing system, especially from the forwards.

Article Title: Cricket Canada appoints Monty Desai as new head coach
Article Text:
Monty Desai (ICC Photo)

Watch Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions Vikram Solanki on GT strategy, pitch & team decisions

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

"Honoured to take on this responsibility"

Cricket Canada has ushered in a new era, appointing Monty Desai as the head coach of the men’s national team, with the board calling it a “pivotal moment” for the country’s cricketing future.Desai, a seasoned coach with over two decades of international experience, brings with him a proven track record in Associate cricket. One of his most notable achievements came with Nepal, where he played a key role in helping the side regain ODI status and qualify for multiple ICC global events.Announcing the appointment, Cricket Canada president Arvinder Khosa stressed the importance of long-term vision and structural growth. “Our foremost priority is to restore Cricket Canada’s prominence through performance, professionalism, and a renewed and vitalized sense of purpose,” Khosa said.“Appointing Monty Desai reflects this vision. His experience with Associate nations, most notably Nepal and Afghanistan, demonstrates his ability to transform teams through effective leadership, discipline, and a clear long term plan,” he added.Khosa also highlighted the timing of the move, especially after Canada’s recent appearance at the ICC Men's T20 World Cup.Desai, who has previously worked with the Afghanistan national cricket team and across India’s domestic and franchise circuits, expressed gratitude and optimism upon taking charge.“I am honoured to take on this responsibility and thank Cricket Canada for their trust,” Desai said. “My previous experience in Canada has given me a clear understanding of the system’s potential and the passion that exists for the game.”Emphasising the rise of Associate nations, he added, “Associate cricket is an important driver in the global growth of cricket today, and teams like Nepal and Afghanistan have demonstrated what belief, discipline, and fearlessness can unlock.”“Canada has a similar opportunity, and my focus will be on building a culture that translates this potential into consistent performances on the international stage,” he said.
Article Summary: Monty desai appointed head coach of canada's men's national team. Desai has over two decades of international experience with nepal and afghanistan. Cricket canada president arvinder khosa stresses importance of long-term vision and structural growth.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Starc vs Sooryavanshi in Jaipur! DC overhaul XI in high-stakes clash vs RR
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Mitchell Starc (Agency Image)

Watch Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more

Starc returns as DC ring changes

RR vs DC Playing XIs:

Delhi Capitals XI:

Rajasthan Royals XI:

NEW DELHI: A marquee battle is set to headline Friday’s IPL 2026 clash as Mitchell Starc makes his season debut for the Delhi Capitals, setting up a mouth-watering contest against teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi when they face the Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur.RR skipper Riyan Parag won the toss and opted to bat first at the Sawai Mansingh Stadium, pointing to surface conditions as a key factor.“We're going to bat first. I think it's not the usual Jaipur wicket. It's a bit dry; we noticed that yesterday and the pitch played a little faster the other day. So hopefully it gets a little slow in the back end, so that's why we're batting first. (On the win against Punjab Kings) I think the middle order and lower order finished it like the way they did and the openers as usual started really brilliantly. But as a collective team effort, chasing down 220 always helps. Everyone's stepping up. That has been the motto, like, as I was saying, we haven't been playing proper and complete cricket for 40 overs. Hopefully we can narrow it down as the tournament reaches the business end. Another show today and we can rest for a few days. Bishnoi comes back in and Shubham is into the main team,” Parag said.The bigger headline, however, came from the DC camp, with Starc finally featuring this season, adding firepower to their pace attack.His duel with Sooryavanshi at the top is expected to set the tone early.DC captain Axar Patel confirmed three changes in the XI as the side looks for a turnaround in a tightening playoff race.“Starc is playing, Chameera isn't playing. Pathum is playing, which means David Miller sits out. Ashok Sharma is there, so there are three changes. We have three changes, the wicket looks good and we're happy to chase. It's important to return to basics. You need to stay in the present to turn things around, focus on the positives. Attitude and body language are very important,” Axar said.With both teams making tweaks and chasing consistency, the encounter carries added significance. But the early exchanges promise the biggest thrill — Starc steaming in with pace and precision, and Sooryavanshi ready to counterattack in what could define the contest.KL Rahul(w), Pathum Nissanka, Nitish Rana, Sameer Rizvi, Tristan Stubbs, Axar Patel(c), Ashutosh Sharma, Kyle Jamieson, Mitchell Starc, Kuldeep Yadav, T NatarajanYashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel(w), Riyan Parag(c), Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Shubham Dubey, Jofra Archer, Ravi Bishnoi, Nandre Burger, Brijesh Sharma
Article Summary: Mitchell starc makes his season debut for delhi capitals against rajasthan royals. Teen sensation vaibhav sooryavanshi set to make his ipl 2026 debut.rr skipper riyan parag won the toss and opted to bat first.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Yashasvi Jaiswal creates first-ball six record but Mitchell Starc has the last laugh
Article Text:
Rajasthan Royals' Yashasvi Jaiswal (ANI Photo)

Watch Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more

Most times, hitting six on the first ball of an IPL innings

Six off the first ball of an IPL match

Yashasvi Jaiswal added another stunning entry to his growing IPL record book with a six off the very first ball against Delhi Capitals in Jaipur. Facing Mitchell Starc, the left-hander made an immediate statement.Starc began short and in the slot, and Jaiswal was quick to react. Hanging back, he picked the length early and swung it cleanly over backward square leg for a massive six. With that hit, Jaiswal moved to the top of the list for most first-ball sixes in IPL innings, achieving the feat for the fourth time.4 - Yashasvi Jaiswal*2 - Priyansh Arya1 - Naman Ojha1 - Mayank Agarwal1 - Sunil Narine1 - Virat Kohli1 - Robin Uthappa1 - Phil Salt1 - Sanju SamsonHe also joined an elite group of players to hit a six off the very first ball of an IPL match, becoming the latest name on that list.Naman Ojha (RR) off Brad Hodge (KKR), Durban, 2009Virat Kohli (RCB) off Varun Aaron (RR), Bengaluru, 2019Phil Salt (KKR) off Nuwan Thushara (MI), Kolkata, 2024Priyansh Arya (PBKS) off Khaleel Ahmed (CSK), Mullanpur, 2025Yashasvi Jaiswal (RR) off Mitchell Starc (DC), Jaipur, 2026*However, the moment was short-lived. Starc responded immediately, dismissing Jaiswal on the third ball.A full toss caught him by surprise, and his attempted swipe ballooned back to the bowler, handing Starc his first wicket of the season. Rajasthan Royals ’ start worsened when Vaibhav Sooryavanshi fell in the next over for just 4, leaving the hosts under early pressure despite the explosive beginning.Earlier, RR captain Riyan Parag had opted to bat, while Starc returned to action after recovering from a shoulder injury.
Article Summary: Yashasvi jaiswal hit a six off the very first ball against delhi capitals in jaipur. The left-hander moved to the top of the list for most first-ball sixes in ipl innings, achieving the feat for the fourth time. He also joined an elite group of players to hit asix off the first ball of an ipl match.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Who is Raghu Sharma? MI’s surprise pick in high-stakes clash vs CSK
Article Text:
Guwahati: Mumbai Indians' Raghu Sharma, right (PTI Photo)

Watch Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more

Punjab cricketer Raghu Sharma found himself in the spotlight during the high-profile IPL clash between Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on May 2. While he did not start in the XI, MI captain Hardik Pandya confirmed at the toss that the 33-year-old leg-spinner would feature as an Impact Player in the second innings. Earlier, Sharma was handed his maiden IPL cap, marking a significant moment in his long domestic journey.Mumbai Indians made another change to their lineup, bringing in Krish Bhagat in place of Ashwani Kumar. Struggling this season with just two wins from eight matches, MI entered the contest placed ninth on the table with four points and a net run rate of -0.784. The fixture also carried added motivation, with MI looking to respond after a heavy 103-run defeat to CSK earlier in the tournament.Sharma’s rise has been built on persistence. Born in Jalandhar, the leg-break bowler has represented Punjab and Puducherry in domestic cricket. Since making his first-class debut in the 2017–18 Ranji Trophy, he has picked up 57 wickets in 12 matches, including five five-wicket hauls and three ten-wicket hauls.He further impressed in the 2024–25 Vijay Hazare Trophy with 14 wickets in eight matches. In T20s, he has taken four wickets in four games at an economy rate of eight since debuting in 2021. His association with MI began as a net bowler before being signed as a mid-season replacement in 2025, with the franchise retaining him as part of their long-term plans.At the toss, Hardik Pandya opted to bat first. For CSK, Ruturaj Gaikwad made two changes, bringing in Ramakrishna Ghosh for Gurjapneet Singh and Prashant Veer in place of Akeal Hosein.
